name: fuzzysearch
summary: On-device 'elastic' search: typo-tolerant fuzzy matching (edit distance) + BM25 relevance ranking over an EDS query-index, fully client-side with no backend.
imports: none (release build; abort is removed by --noAssert)

model.fuzzy: Each query term is expanded over the vocabulary with restricted Damerau–Levenshtein (Optimal String Alignment) edit distance within an Elasticsearch-style AUTO fuzziness (0 edits for len<=2, 1 for len<=5, 2 otherwise), capped by maxEdits. Length filter + row-minimum early exit prune non-matches.
model.bm25: score(D) = sum over query terms of max over matched vocab terms of [ fuzzyBoost * idf(t) * tf(t,D)*(k1+1) / (tf(t,D) + k1*(1 - b + b*|D|/avgdl)) ], where fuzzyBoost = 1 - editDistance/(maxEdits+1) and idf(t) = ln(1 + (N - df(t) + 0.5)/(df(t) + 0.5)). Taking the max matched term per query term per document avoids double-counting near-duplicate vocab terms.
model.idf: idf is a pure function of df(t) and N, both known at index-build time, so the host precomputes idf[t] once; the kernel reads it (no transcendental math in the kernel, and the JS twin shares the same table).

memory.note: linear memory, exported for host marshaling

reset.desc: Rewind the bump allocator before laying out a fresh index.

alloc.desc: Reserve byteLen bytes (8-byte aligned), growing memory as needed.

params.name: idxPtr
params.desc: byte ptr to the 56-byte index header (see memoryLayout.header)

params.name: qCharsPtr
params.desc: byte ptr to u16 query-term chars, concatenated

params.name: qOffsetsPtr
params.desc: byte ptr to i32 offsets[qTermCount+1] (u16 units) per query term

params.name: bestPtr
params.desc: scratch: f32[docCount] best-matched-term contribution per doc

scoreAll.desc: Writes one f32 BM25 score per document (>0 match, 0 = no match). Used by the benchmark to check WASM == JS; the shipped block uses searchTopK.

params.name: outPtr
params.desc: byte ptr to {i32 doc; f32 score}[k] (OUTPUT), best first

searchTopK.desc: Fused fuzzy-BM25 score + bounded top-k selection. Ranks entirely in WASM (score desc, ties by lower doc index) and returns only the winners, so no full scores array or JS-side sort touches the main thread. This is the block's per-keystroke path.

memoryLayout.name: vocabChars
memoryLayout.desc: All unique terms' lowercased char codes concatenated. Written once at init.

memoryLayout.name: vocabOffsets
memoryLayout.desc: Start index (in u16 units) of each vocab term.

memoryLayout.name: postOffsets / postDocs / postTf
memoryLayout.desc: CSR inverted index: term t's postings are [postOffsets[t], postOffsets[t+1]); each posting is (postDocs[p] docId, postTf[p] term frequency). df(t) = postOffsets[t+1]-postOffsets[t].

memoryLayout.name: idf
memoryLayout.desc: Precomputed inverse document frequency per term (index metadata). Both the kernel and the JS twin read this same table.

memoryLayout.name: docLen
memoryLayout.desc: Token length of each document (BM25 length normalization).

memoryLayout.name: query
memoryLayout.desc: Tokenized query terms, written every keystroke.

memoryLayout.name: scores
memoryLayout.desc: One BM25 score per document. scoreAll returns it; searchTopK uses it as scratch and returns only the top-k.

memoryLayout.name: topk
memoryLayout.desc: searchTopK output: the top-k documents, best first. The host reads only the even (doc) slots.

marshaling.rule: Call reset(), then alloc() every buffer up front (alloc may grow memory and detach views), then create typed-array views over memory.buffer, then write the index + header. After init no re-grow happens, so per-keystroke calls reuse the same views.
marshaling.boundaryCrossings: 1 wasm call per keystroke. The block calls searchTopK, which returns only the top-k doc indices; the full scores buffer never crosses back to JS.
